pr.title: Bring over va_arg from SMB decomp
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced the handwritten assembly object for Runtime __va_arg with a C implementation brought from the SMB decompilation, added a project stdarg.h defining va_list and va_start/va_arg/va_end support, and updated obj_files.mk to build src/Runtime/__va_arg.o instead of asm/Runtime/__va_arg.o. The PR body says this was added preemptively because multiple future matching targets were expected to need va_arg.
what_changed: Deleted asm/Runtime/__va_arg.s, removing the 74-line assembly implementation of __va_arg.
Added src/Runtime/__va_arg.c with a 56-line C implementation of void *__va_arg(va_list v_list, unsigned char type).
Added include/stdarg.h with __va_list/va_list definitions, a non-MWERKS declaration for __builtin_va_info, a prototype for __va_arg, and macros for va_start, va_arg, and va_end.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES now references $(BUILD_DIR)/src/Runtime/__va_arg.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/Runtime/__va_arg.o.

The C implementation preserves the observed runtime behavior from the assembly: type 4 uses 16-byte alignment and advances input_arg_area by 0x10; type 3 switches from gpr to fpr handling and uses an FPR save-area offset of 32; type 2 handles 8-byte values with even GPR alignment; type 0 dereferences the computed argument address before returning it.
smart_moves: Used an existing solution from the SMB decompilation rather than re-deriving __va_arg from scratch, reducing risk for a low-level ABI/runtime helper.
Moved __va_arg from asm to C while keeping the build graph stable by changing only the object path in obj_files.mk.
Added stdarg.h at the same time as __va_arg.c, making the runtime helper usable by future C decompilation work that needs variadic functions.
Encoded alignment logic with a small ALIGN macro using uintptr_t, matching the pointer arithmetic needs of the original assembly while keeping the C code readable.
Modeled the compiler ABI state explicitly in __va_list using gpr, fpr, input_arg_area, and reg_save_area fields, which mirrors how the assembly manipulated the va_list state.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling variadic functions, add the runtime/header support first so later matching work can use va_start and va_arg naturally instead of preserving awkward assembly or stubs.
For runtime support code, replacing an asm object with a C object requires both the source file and the object list update; otherwise the old asm object may continue to be built.

The PowerPC/MWCC va_list layout used here stores GPR and FPR cursor bytes plus pointers to the input argument area and register save area; future code using stdarg should rely on this shared header instead of redefining it locally.

The __va_arg implementation distinguishes argument classes by the unsigned char type value passed from _var_arg_typeof(t); future matching should preserve those type cases rather than treating va_arg as generic pointer arithmetic.
8-byte GPR arguments need even-register handling, and stack fallback needs explicit alignment before advancing input_arg_area.
Weak evidence note: the PR has no review comments, so no reviewer rationale or style feedback is available beyond the title, body, and diff.
naming_conventions: Runtime source path mirrors the former asm path: asm/Runtime/__va_arg.s became src/Runtime/__va_arg.c.
The header uses include/stdarg.h with guard _STDARG_H_.
The internal va_list array typedef is named __va_list, with public typedef va_list.
The va_arg helper is named __va_arg and takes parameters named v_list and type.
Local variables in __va_arg.c use ABI-descriptive names: g_reg, maxsize, increment, even, fpr_offset, regsize, input_arg_area, and reg_save_area.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recreated an assembly runtime helper in C and switched obj_files.mk from the asm object to the src object as the matching milestone.
Preserved assembly-visible control flow concepts as C cases based on type values 0, 2, 3, and 4.
Used ALIGN(addr, size) as (((uintptr_t)(addr) + ((size)-1)) & ~((size)-1)) to reproduce stack/input argument alignment.
For type 4, aligned input_arg_area to 16 bytes and advanced by 0x10, matching the old assembly path that rounded with 0xF and masked.
For FPR handling, selected &v_list->fpr, used v_list->fpr as the register index, set size to 8, fpr_offset to 32, and regsize to 8.
For 8-byte GPR handling, reduced maxsize from 8 to 7, set increment to 2, and conditionally advanced odd register indices to even alignment.
For register-save-area arguments, computed addr from reg_save_area plus fpr_offset plus g_reg * regsize, then wrote back the advanced register cursor.
For stack fallback, forced the relevant register cursor to 8, aligned input_arg_area to the argument size, and advanced input_arg_area by size.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or functions that began using include/stdarg.h, va_start, or va_arg after PR #257.
Check whether __va_arg.c stayed matching as compiler settings or include/stdarg.h evolved.
Look for future refinements to the va_list layout, _var_arg_typeof usage, or MWERKS/non-MWERKS handling.
Compare this __va_arg implementation against SMB decomp source if provenance or exact ABI behavior needs confirmation.
